what_it_does: Loop-prep document 2 of 3 for the 'coherence lane' build: it specifies the HOW — the build sequence, dos/don'ts, file territory, and dependency-first build order — for an automated build loop (cron-picked) that constructs a coherence/detection/disposition substrate inside the Company codebase.
observations: Names itself doc 2 of 3, pairing with COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md (the WHAT) and a Research Synthesis triad (COHERENCE-SUBSTRATE.md / SEMANTIC-LAYER.md / CORPUS-CHAIN.md plus findings/).

Lists 'Standing laws' that bind every step: registry-is-truth/no-hardcoding, additive (bump schema_ver), fail-loud, reflects-never-owns, own/reflect, positive-only (proposes never auto-acts), operator-only floor, verify-by-use, claim shared files in WORK-SPLIT.md before editing, 'company suites' green before shared-file commit.
Declares a read-vs-mutate boundary: this lane builds detectors/gates/substrate but never touches the engine (cognition.py / run_items / run_reduce) or the FE.

Enumerates explicit file territory: runtime/coherence_detect.py, runtime/suite.py gate methods (~7025-7174), several new *_acceptance.py tests, design/_system registries, and store/fs_store.py finding/disposition methods.

Organized into Groups A-F; A is 'already built', B assembles a shared pre-commit suite, C the disposition store + finding model, D the calibration harness, E chains/graphs as saveable actions, F the convergence-round lens.

Group C distinguishes DETECTION (re-derivable, never owned) from DISPOSITION (a decision, the one genuinely persisted thing — 'a micro-ADR'), and reuses existing primitives: append_event/_emit, pin-overlay-last-wins, run_stats read-time-rollup, Inbox status-lane shape.

Group D frames the calibration harness as 'experiment with the LLM layer + save what works' (attributed to Tim): run a detector under N model configs against a labelled eval set → precision/recall/cost/latency → save winning config.

Group E builds only the SAVING side of chains/graphs-as-actions (declare/validate/save/register), handing the runner seam to cognition; warns against deciding the Action schema unilaterally and to co-design it in a 'coordination home'.
Ends with an explicit build order for the cron and an 'engine-independent prefix' list of what is buildable now versus what waits on cognition's engine.
standouts: The own/reflect distinction: detection rides existing machinery (re-derivable), only disposition is persisted as a mutable overlay keyed by finding-id rather than mutating the append-only record.
Calibration harness reframed as turning trust-tiering 'from assertion into a measured number per class'.
C6 'the recursion' — migrating the declared _ORPHAN_ROUTES registry into finding-records so the catalogue becomes the system's own first findings.
Heavy reuse mandate: every new field/behavior is mapped to an existing primitive rather than building new storage or a second pre-commit mechanism.
conventions: Follows a loop-prep triad convention (criteria + guide + synthesis) and references file:line evidence kept in the synthesis rather than duplicated here.
Uses status markers (✅ built, 🟡 net-new/pending) and AREA/SEM/CC/criterion codes (AREA-6, SEM-3, SEM-4, CC-1, C6, D4, E2) as cross-references into companion docs.
Per-criterion workflow convention: build in territory → verify by use → suites green → claim-release + commit per criterion → mark criterion.
Adheres to the repo's registry-is-truth / additive / fail-loud / verify-by-use standing laws explicitly.
concerns: Extremely dense and reference-heavy (codes like AREA-6/SEM-4/CC-1, commit hashes) that are opaque without the companion docs — meaning lives outside this file.
Group A status is asserted as built with commit hashes but the document itself cannot confirm current build-state.
notes: This is the procedural playbook for an autonomous coherence-substrate build loop — read it as the sequencing/boundaries doc, with the actual reasoning and evidence living in its companion synthesis files.
questions: What exactly are the structural vs semantic detectors, and what does coherence_detect.py currently contain?
What is the 'coordination home' where the Action schema is to be co-designed?
What is the precise current ✅/🟡 state of each group, given the doc asserts A is built?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it states it is the HOW/build-sequence loop-prep doc and its body is exactly that: laws, file territory, per-group sequences, and a dependency-ordered build order.
apparent_themes: Separation of re-derivable detection from persisted decision (own/reflect)
Lane discipline and co-design boundaries between parallel build loops
intention_signals: It is trying to make a complex, multi-lane build executable by an autonomous loop without contaminating sibling lanes — by hard-bounding file territory, encoding standing laws, and ordering work dependency-first so the cron can pick the next buildable criterion safely.
novelty: The 'disposition as micro-ADR' framing and the recursion of migrating a hardcoded orphan-routes registry into the very finding-store it is building are distinctive structural moves.
contribution: Contributes the coherence lane's build methodology: the detection/disposition substrate design, the reuse-mapping of new behaviors onto existing store primitives, the calibration-harness-as-measurement concept, and the lane-boundary/build-order discipline a fused system would keep as the coherence subsystem's implementation playbook.
summary_for_embedding: A loop-prep implementation guide specifying the build sequence, file territory, standing laws, and dependency order for the Company's coherence detection/disposition substrate, calibration harness, and saveable-action declaration layer.
intention_for_embedding: To let an autonomous build loop construct the coherence substrate safely and reuse-first, without touching the engine or FE, by encoding boundaries, per-group sequences, and a dependency-first build order.
